justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified — 'No request ID / correlation ID in any log line'. (2) API call duration gap identified — 'No duration of the SmartyStreets call logged'. (3) Metric label gap identified — 'validation_requests_total lacks result label (valid/invalid/error)'. (4) Latency histogram absence noted — 'No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds)'. (5) PII consideration flagged for input address — listed as a gap under data_flow_integrity but NOT blindly recommended; the integration assessment notes it neutrally ('Input address is not logged') and the observability review lists it as a gap for reproducibility, without demanding it be logged unconditionally. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are specific and actionable throughout — e.g. 'Add result label to validation_requests_total', 'Add Prometheus histogram validation_duration_seconds', 'Add correlation ID to all log lines'.

observations.text: All four core observability gaps (request ID, duration, metric labels, latency histogram) explicitly identified with clear explanations of operational impact
Verdict is definitively NOT READY with well-structured escalations routed to Coach K
Recommendations are highly specific and actionable — names exact metric labels, histogram names, and configuration changes
Goes beyond minimum rubric requirements: identifies tracing gaps, health check anti-pattern, circuit breaker absence, and multiple failure scenarios
PII handling is appropriately nuanced — flags missing input address logging as a data flow integrity concern for reproducibility but does not blindly mandate logging PII
Debugging capability summary is excellent — paints a vivid on-call scenario showing why the gaps matter operationally

PII consideration could be more explicitly called out as a decision point rather than just listed as a gap — the word 'PII' does not appear in the output, though the concern is implicitly handled by not recommending blanket logging of addresses
